_doc: Project invariants enforced by .claude/hooks/invariant_guard.py (PreToolUse). Add an entry when the user gives a durable rule that future edits must respect (e.g. 'list views must always sort by type'). The hook reads this file on every Edit/Write/MultiEdit and BLOCKS the tool call if a `blocker` severity invariant is being violated. The 5 defaults shipped v0.22+ are conservative (severity=warn → informational only) and meant as demo/seed value out-of-box; DEV adds project-specific rules via /inv-add and may promote any default to `blocker` per project policy.

_schema.applies_to: list of glob patterns (workspace-relative). Empty = applies everywhere.

rules.must_keep_regex: list of regex patterns; if old_string matched, new_string must too
rules.must_keep_call: list of bare function/attribute names; matches `name(` and `.name(`
rules.deny_write_glob: list of globs (v0.34) — DENY any Edit/Write whose TARGET PATH matches, regardless of content. For files only the harness / DEV may author (e.g. sub-agent transcripts). Fires independently of must_keep_* rules.

_schema.severity: blocker (deny edit) or warn (allow + inject reminder)
_schema.rationale: why this rule exists — surface in the block message

_workflow: 1. User states a durable rule: 'sort theo type cố định'.
2. Run /adr-add to capture the WHY in decision-log.md.
3. Run /inv-add to translate the rule into must_keep_regex / must_keep_call patterns.
5. The hook now BLOCKS any edit that strips the pattern.

invariants.description: Helper scripts must invoke Python via env / venv / sys.executable — never a bare `python` shebang.
invariants.rationale: A bare `python` shebang resolves to whatever Python is first on PATH (often system Python missing the project's deps). Helper scripts must pin to `#!/usr/bin/env python3`, the venv's `python.exe`, or call through `sys.executable`. Fix when violated: restore the explicit interpreter reference that the edit dropped.
invariants._doc: Default shipped v0.22+. Promoted warn → blocker in v0.26.0 (audit follow-up): bare-shebang is unambiguously broken, no false-positive risk, matches every Odoo consumer's `feedback_python_venv` rule.

invariants.description: Auth/credential flow docs and configs must reference the gitignored `.codex/mcp.local.env` channel or the `credential_guard` precommit, not inline secrets.
invariants.rationale: Credentials policy (feedback_credentials): real creds live ONLY in `.codex/mcp.local.env` (gitignored), surfaced by the `credential_guard` precommit hook. If an edit strips both references from an auth-flow file, the doc no longer points to the safe channel and DEV may inline secrets. Fix when violated: restore the `.codex/mcp.local.env` or `credential_guard` reference.
invariants._doc: Default shipped v0.22+. Promoted warn → blocker in v0.26.0 (audit follow-up): inline secret risk is high-severity + this regex is positive-match (false-positive impossible). Note: `must_keep_regex` cannot express must-NOT-match, so this invariant guards the POSITIVE reference. Use a separate precommit / secret-scanner for the negative (no-inline-passwords) check.

invariants.description: Creates / writes on company-scoped Odoo models must thread the company context through `with_company()` (v14+) or `with_context(force_company=)` / `allowed_company_ids` (v12-13).
invariants.rationale: Without an explicit company context, `create()` silently falls back to `self.env.company` (the user's *current* switcher selection), cross-pollinating data between companies. See `odoo-multi-company` skill §1. Fix when violated: restore the `.with_company(rec.company_id)` chain (v17) or `.with_context(force_company=rec.company_id.id)` (v12) that the edit dropped.
invariants._doc: Default shipped v0.22+. Promoted warn → blocker in v0.27.0 (P2 audit 2026-05-28): regex is highly specific (`.with_company(X).create/write` OR `with_context(force_company/allowed_company_ids)`), no false-positive candidate found in a 198-module production addon scan. Sourced from `templates/cursor/skills/odoo/odoo-multi-company/SKILL.md` §1 (Agent G's invariant suggestion). Version-aware regex covers both v12 (force_company) and v14+ (with_company) idioms.

invariants.description: Odoo test files must use a proper Odoo test base class (`TransactionCase` / `SavepointCase` / `HttpCase` / `TestRendering`) or pytest fixture / `@tagged` decorator — bare `def test_*()` functions don't get DB setup.
invariants.rationale: Bare `def test_foo()` functions in Odoo addons silently skip — `odoo-bin -i <mod> --test-enable` only picks up classes inheriting an Odoo test base (or pytest-collected fixtures). Removing the base-class declaration / `@tagged` decorator silently disables the whole test file. Fix when violated: restore the test base class or `@tagged(...)` decorator that the edit dropped.
invariants._doc: Default shipped v0.22+. Promoted warn → blocker in v0.27.0 (P2 audit 2026-05-28): regex covers all 5 standard Odoo test bases + pytest fixtures + @tagged decorator; a production codebase scan found 0 custom `XxxTestCase` extending non-standard parents (all real tests use TransactionCase / SavepointCase / @tagged). Aligned with `odoo-12-backend.mdc` testing guidance + Odoo upstream test-discovery semantics.

invariants.description: Every `.sudo()` call must carry an adjacent comment justifying why the access-rule bypass is safe.
invariants.rationale: `sudo()` bypasses `ir.rule` + `ir.model.access` — the most common Odoo security finding in code review is an undocumented sudo whose intent is forgotten by the next maintainer. Toolkit rule `odoo-12-backend.mdc`: 'Document every sudo() with a short reason.' Fix when violated: restore the `# sudo: <reason>` comment that the edit dropped, or remove the sudo entirely if the rationale no longer applies.
invariants._doc: Default shipped v0.22+. Confidence M — regex catches the common 'sudo() # reason' / 'sudo()\n# reason' / docstring patterns but a sudo on its own line with the comment 5 lines away would slip; project may promote to blocker with a stricter sibling check via /probe-add.
invariants._audit_2026_05_28: REVIEWED P2: KEEP at warn. Real-codebase scan (a 198-module production deployment) found 1037 `.sudo()` calls in models/wizards/controllers, the vast majority WITHOUT adjacent comment — meaning if any edit happens in those files, the `must_keep_regex` won't match old_string (no sudo+comment context to preserve), so promotion to blocker would have no effect on existing files. Worse, the broad `#[^\n]*sudo` OR-branch matches ANY comment containing the word 'sudo' (e.g., 'sudo apt install' in shell-snippet docstrings), creating false-positive risk. To make this invariant effective, redesign regex to require `.sudo()` adjacency (within ±3 lines of the comment) — track as follow-up.

invariants.description: The agent must never WRITE a sub-agent transcript — `**/.claude/projects/**/subagents/*.jsonl` files are HARNESS-authored and are the un-forgeable evidence the independent-review gate consumes; writing one forges a reviewer.
invariants.rationale: independent_review_gate (v0.31) trusts sub-agent transcripts at <proj>/<session>/subagents/*.jsonl as proof a fresh-context reviewer ran. The harness writes them on a real Task spawn (confirmed empirically v0.34 T8a) — the agent never needs to Write there. Denying agent Write closes the forge-a-reviewer hole (F4.1) AND protects the reviewer-authored verdict: F4.2 reads the reviewer's `REVIEW-VERDICT: <sha> PASS|FAIL` line FROM this same harness-written transcript, so denying agent Write here is exactly what makes that verdict un-forgeable (no separate verdict file exists — the verdict travels in the transcript). The broad `**/subagents/**` is a review round-1 add so a RELATIVE `.claude/...subagents/...` path (which the strict glob's leading-slash assumption can miss) is still denied.
invariants._doc: v0.34 F4.1+F4.2 (T8/T9), uses the deny_write_glob rule-type added to invariant_guard in T8b. Ships block-default (severity blocker) — invariant_guard is itself a PreToolUse DENY hook, and there is NO legitimate reason for the agent to Write a subagents/*.jsonl file, so false-positive risk is ~0 (the only v0.34 trigger that ships enforcing rather than warn-capable). One-shot DEV override remains `bypass-invariant: no-subagents-forge`. NB: the throwaway-echo-bot forge (a sub-agent that merely echoes the public packet-sha + a PASS line without reviewing) is NOT closed by this — that residual needs a per-Stop nonce / crypto-signed verdict, deferred to v0.35.
